directive:
D0 MAXIMALITY DIRECTIVE — REOPEN AND COMPLETE THE FIXED-INTERIOR MATCHED MINIMAX KERNEL. Do not bank the epsilon=1/2 obstruction as the headline. Faithfully correct the theorem domain to “for every fixed 0<epsilon<1/2,” explicitly recording that this removes only the degenerate randomized endpoint and does not strengthen overlap otherwise; do not launder the correction as a new substantive assumption.

Restore lem:light-cell-polynomial as a reachable, displayed result and re-prove it at publication standard. Use independent fixed-size pilot and estimation blocks m0,m1; set M=floor(alpha_epsilon log(en)) and B=b log(en)/m1; define H_M(x)={1-T_M(1-2x)}/(2M^2), E_M(x)=H_M(x)/x, G_M(x)={1-E_M(x)}/x, and
P_{M,B}(u)=s(u)t_1(u)G_M(s_1(u)/B)/B-s(u)t_0(u)G_M(s_0(u)/B)/B.
Lift each monomial u^r by prod_j(N_j)_{r_j}/(m1)_{|r|}. Prove, rather than assert: (i) the polynomial identities and the O_epsilon(B/M^2) overlap-cone approximation; (ii) an explicit shifted-Chebyshev coefficient formula and scaled multivariate l1 coefficient bound; (iii) same-category and cross-category fixed-multinomial factorial-product/covariance lemmas with all falling-factorial ratio conditions; (iv) the shifted-argument coefficient bounds used in the variance estimate; (v) a complete second-moment bound outside p_k<=B; (vi) pilot false-light control; (vii) conditioning for the random selected set; and (viii) uniform normalization and the finite-n cases. If fixed-multinomial bookkeeping remains opaque, prove the lemma first under independent Poisson counts and supply an explicit de-Poissonization lemma with no rate loss.

Replace the cone-projection heavy handle by the concrete ratio plug-in
hhat_k=(N_k/m1)[N_{1k1}/N_{1k} 1{N_{1k}>0}-N_{0k1}/N_{0k} 1{N_{0k}>0}],
and define That_H=sum_{k in Hhat} hhat_k. Use a pilot threshold t log(en) with constants B_-<B_+ both of order log n/n and prove a pilot-sandwich lemma: outside probability O(n^{-K}), every selected-heavy cell has p_k>=B_- and every selected-light cell has p_k<=B_+. Prove for every deterministic H subset {k:p_k>=B_-} the exact heavy aggregation bound
E[(That_H-sum_{k in H}phi(q_k))^2]<=C_epsilon/n,
including the missing-arm bias sum_k p_k exp(-c_epsilon m1 p_k), inverse-binomial or Poissonized variance, multinomial cross-cell covariance, and uniform control of false-heavy cells. Combine the heavy and repaired light lemmas, account for their covariance by sample independence or a stated inequality, and truncate only the final estimator to [-1,1].

Then state and prove the interior theorem
R_{n,d,epsilon} asy_epsilon 1/n+d^2/(n^2 log^2 n)
uniformly for d<=c_epsilon n log n. Map the published moment-matching lower bound precisely into this model: verify its strict-interior epsilon quantifier, ATE versus one-arm functional reduction, binary-outcome construction, Poissonization/de-Poissonization, constants, normalization, and d-range. Derive explicitly the parametric elbow d=O(sqrt(n) log n) and consistency iff d=o(n log n) within the proved range.

Retain epsilon=1/2 only as a separate boundary theorem, with an independent parametric lower bound and the linear estimator upper bound. Qualify prop:two-category-confounding: it is a confounding witness only for epsilon<1/2 and degenerates to randomization at epsilon=1/2. Add the near-endpoint lemma with delta=1/2-epsilon:
R_{n,d,epsilon}<=4/n+16 delta^2,
and, after combining estimators, record the upper envelope 1/n+min{d^2/(n^2 log^2 n),delta^2}; label any matching triangular-array epsilon_n phase lower bound open unless actually proved.

Do not mark the interior theorem or light lemma proved until every listed obligation is emitted in the result graph with its own proof and dependencies. Synchronize tldr, project_justification gap/niche/fill, related_work, interpretation, honest_scope, technical_internal_limitation, every statement_notes entry, and results-at-a-glance to the corrected fixed-interior headline, the separate randomized endpoint, the actual proof status of the light/heavy lemmas, and the near-endpoint phase statement. Remove all prose saying the light lemma is proved while it is absent, orphaned, or still has an open obligation.

changed.from: Split \(\{1,\ldots,n\}\) independently into \(I_0\) and \(I_1\) of sizes \(\lfloor n/2\rfloor\) and \(n-\lfloor n/2\rfloor\). Use the pilot totals \(\sum_{a,y}N^{(0)}_{aky}\) to form a heavy set \(\widehat{\mathcal H}_n\) and its complement \(\widehat{\mathcal L}_n\). On \(\widehat{\mathcal H}_n\), evaluate \(\phi\) at the cone projection of normalized second-split counts. On \(\widehat{\mathcal L}_n\), approximate \(\phi\) on \(\mathcal C_\epsilon\) by a bounded multivariate polynomial and estimate every monomial by its second-split factorial moment \(\prod_{a,y}(N^{(1)}_{aky})_{r_{ay}}/(|I_1|)_{\sum_{a,y}r_{ay}}\). Add the two contributions and truncate the result to \([-1,1]\), calling it \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}\). This heavy/light split, cone approximation, and factorial-moment construction are the handle for \(oeq:sharp-minimax\).
changed.to: Let \(m_j=|I_j|\), \(L=\log(en)\), and fix \(t_\epsilon,b_\epsilon>0\) and a universal \(\alpha>0\), with \(b_\epsilon\ge16t_\epsilon\) and \(2\alpha\log A<1/4\), where \(A\) is the absolute coefficient constant in \(lem:chebyshev-weighted-reciprocal\). For all sufficiently large \(n\), set \(M=\max\{2,\lfloor\alpha L\rfloor\}\), \(B=b_\epsilon L/m_1\), \(\widehat{\mathcal H}_n=\{k:\sum_{a,y}N^{(0)}_{aky}>t_\epsilon L\}\), and \(\widehat{\mathcal L}_n=\widehat{\mathcal H}_n^c\). Put \(N_k^{(1)}=\sum_{a,y}N^{(1)}_{aky}\), \(N_{ak}^{(1)}=\sum_yN^{(1)}_{aky}\), and \[\widehat h_k=\frac{N_k^{(1)}}{m_1}\left\{\frac{N^{(1)}_{1k1}}{N^{(1)}_{1k}}\mathbf1\{N^{(1)}_{1k}>0\}-\frac{N^{(1)}_{0k1}}{N^{(1)}_{0k}}\mathbf1\{N^{(1)}_{0k}>0\}\right\}.\] Define \(H_M(x)=\{1-T_M(1-2x)\}/(2M^2)\), \(E_M(x)=H_M(x)/x\), \(G_M(x)=\{1-E_M(x)\}/x\), and \[P_{M,B}(u)=\frac{s(u)t_1(u)}B G_M\!\left(\frac{s_1(u)}B\right)-\frac{s(u)t_0(u)}B G_M\!\left(\frac{s_0(u)}B\right),\] where \(s_a(u)=u_{a0}+u_{a1}\), \(s=s_0+s_1\), \(t_1=u_{11}\), and \(t_0=u_{01}\). Expand \(P_{M,B}(u)=\sum_{|r|\le M}c_ru^r\), put \[\widehat P_k=\sum_{|r|\le M}c_r\frac{\prod_{a,y}(N^{(1)}_{aky})_{r_{ay}}}{(m_1)_{|r|}},\quad \widehat T_{\mathcal H}=\sum_{k\in\widehat{\mathcal H}_n}\widehat h_k,\quad \widehat T_{\mathcal L}=\sum_{k\in\widehat{\mathcal L}_n}\widehat P_k,\] and define \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}=\max\{-1,\min(1,\widehat T_{\mathcal H}+\widehat T_{\mathcal L})\}\). For the finitely many smaller \(n\), set \(\widehat{\mathcal L}_n=\varnothing\) and use the ratio branch on every category. The coefficient formula makes the estimator computable in \(O(dM^4)\) arithmetic operations.
changed.reason: The frozen heavy branch is a cone projection, but the required aggregate proof is for the concrete ratio plug-in. The correction also records the exact Chebyshev polynomial, factorial normalization, fixed-overlap tuning, finite-sample convention, and complexity.

{"id":"def:hybrid-estimator-handle","name":"universally tuned balanced two-split ratio-polynomial hybrid estimator","construction":"Set \(I_0=\{1,\ldots,\lfloor n/2\rfloor\}\), \(I_1=\{\lfloor n/2\rfloor+1,\ldots,n\}\), \(m_j=|I_j|\), and \(L=\log(en)\). Let \(A>1\) be the absolute coefficient constant in \(lem:chebyshev-weighted-reciprocal\), put \(\lambda_0=256\), \(b_0=4096\), \(D_A=8\log(9A/8)\), and
\[
\alpha_0=\min\left\{1,\frac1{32\log A},\frac1{256D_A}\right\}.
\]
For every \(n\ge2\), put \(M(n)=\max\{2,\lfloor\alpha_0L\rfloor\}\) and \(B(n)=b_0L/m_1\). Let \(N_0\) be the least numerical cutoff such that, for every \(n\ge N_0\), \(4M(n)^2\le m_1\), \(4M(n)/m_1\le3B(n)/4\), and \(2D_A\le L/256\). For \(n\ge N_0\), abbreviate \(M=M(n)\), \(B=B(n)\), and set
\[
\widehat{\mathcal H}_n=\left\{k:\sum_{a,y}N^{(0)}_{aky}>\lambda_0L\right\},
\qquad
\widehat{\mathcal L}_n=\widehat{\mathcal H}_n^c.
\]
Put \(N_k^{(1)}=\sum_{a,y}N^{(1)}_{aky}\), \(N_{ak}^{(1)}=\sum_yN^{(1)}_{aky}\), and
\[
\widehat h_k=\frac{N_k^{(1)}}{m_1}\left\{
\frac{N^{(1)}_{1k1}}{N^{(1)}_{1k}}\mathbf1\{N^{(1)}_{1k}>0\}
-\frac{N^{(1)}_{0k1}}{N^{(1)}_{0k}}\mathbf1\{N^{(1)}_{0k}>0\}
\right\}.
\]
Define \(H_M(x)=\{1-T_M(1-2x)\}/(2M^2)\), \(E_M(x)=H_M(x)/x\), \(G_M(x)=\{1-E_M(x)\}/x\), and
\[
P_{M,B}(u)=\frac{s(u)t_1(u)}B G_M\!\left(\frac{s_1(u)}B\right)
-\frac{s(u)t_0(u)}B G_M\!\left(\frac{s_0(u)}B\right),
\]
where \(s_a(u)=u_{a0}+u_{a1}\), \(s=s_0+s_1\), \(t_1(u)=u_{11}\), and \(t_0(u)=u_{01}\). Expand \(P_{M,B}(u)=\sum_{|r|\le M}c_ru^r\) and put
\[
\widehat P_k=\sum_{|r|\le M}c_r
\frac{\prod_{a,y}(N^{(1)}_{aky})_{r_{ay}}}{(m_1)_{|r|}},
\quad
\widehat T_{\mathcal H}=\sum_{k\in\widehat{\mathcal H}_n}\widehat h_k,
\quad
\widehat T_{\mathcal L}=\sum_{k\in\widehat{\mathcal L}_n}\widehat P_k.
\]
Finally define
\[
\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}
=\max\{-1,\min(1,\widehat T_{\mathcal H}+\widehat T_{\mathcal L})\}.

\alpha_0=\min\left\{1,\frac1{32\log A},\frac1{256D_A}\right\}.
\]
For \(n\ge2\), put \(M(n)=\max\{2,\lfloor\alpha_0L\rfloor\}\) and \(B(n)=b_0L/m_1\). Let \(N_0\) be the least numerical cutoff such that, for every \(n\ge N_0\), \(\alpha_0L\ge2\), \(4M(n)^2\le m_1\), and \(4M(n)/m_1\le3B(n)/4\). For \(n\ge N_0\), abbreviate \(M=M(n)\), \(B=B(n)\), and set
\[
\widehat{\mathcal H}_n=\left\{k:\sum_{a,y}N^{(0)}_{aky}>\lambda_0L\right\},\qquad \widehat{\mathcal L}_n=\widehat{\mathcal H}_n^c.
\]
Put \(N_k^{(1)}=\sum_{a,y}N^{(1)}_{aky}\), \(N_{ak}^{(1)}=\sum_yN^{(1)}_{aky}\), and
\[
\widehat h_k=\frac{N_k^{(1)}}{m_1}\left\{\frac{N^{(1)}_{1k1}}{N^{(1)}_{1k}}\mathbf1\{N^{(1)}_{1k}>0\}-\frac{N^{(1)}_{0k1}}{N^{(1)}_{0k}}\mathbf1\{N^{(1)}_{0k}>0\}\right\}.
\]
Define \(H_M(x)=\{1-T_M(1-2x)\}/(2M^2)\), \(E_M(x)=H_M(x)/x\), and \(G_M(x)=\{1-E_M(x)\}/x\), using polynomial continuation at zero, and
\[
P_{M,B}(u)=\frac{s(u)t_1(u)}B G_M\!\left(\frac{s_1(u)}B\right)-\frac{s(u)t_0(u)}B G_M\!\left(\frac{s_0(u)}B\right).
\]
Here \(s_a(u)=u_{a0}+u_{a1}\), \(s(u)=s_0(u)+s_1(u)\), \(t_1(u)=u_{11}\), and \(t_0(u)=u_{01}\). Expand \(P_{M,B}(u)=\sum_{|r|\le M}c_ru^r\), set
\[
\widehat P_k=\sum_{|r|\le M}c_r\frac{\prod_{a,y}(N^{(1)}_{aky})_{r_{ay}}}{(m_1)_{|r|}},\quad \widehat T_{\mathcal H}=\sum_{k\in\widehat{\mathcal H}_n}\widehat h_k,\quad \widehat T_{\mathcal L}=\sum_{k\in\widehat{\mathcal L}_n}\widehat P_k,
\]
and define \(\widehat\tau_n^{\mathrm{hyb}}=\max\{-1,\min(1,\widehat T_{\mathcal H}+\widehat T_{\mathcal L})\}\). For \(n<N_0\), set \(\widehat{\mathcal L}_n=\varnothing\) and use the ratio branch on every category. The estimator uses no overlap constant and is computable in \(O(dM^4)\) arithmetic operations.
